hypothesis: Incorporating protein flexibility through ensemble pharmacophore modeling—derived from multiple receptor conformations rather than a single crystal structure—as the reference set for the CATS-guided reward function will yield generated molecules with broader selectivity profiles and improved experimental binding affinities compared to pharmacophores extracted from a single static structure, because the ensemble captures the dynamic interaction landscape of the binding site and thus encodes a richer set of pharmacophoric constraints that better predict activity across conformationally diverse receptor states.
